hypothesis: Using an uncertainty-maximizing acquisition (selecting the image with highest predictive epistemic uncertainty along the model-predicted NEB path) in NN-BAX will reduce the number of expensive DFT energy/force evaluations required for NEB convergence by at least 30% compared to the random-path-sampling NN-BAX used in this paper for systems of 50–150 atoms, while keeping predicted energy barriers within 5% of classical NEB.
